tests.test_body:

; CHECK-LABEL: define i32 @add_sext_icmp_multi_use_add2(
; CHECK-SAME: i32 [[A:%.*]]) {
; CHECK-NEXT:    [[ADD2:%.*]] = call i32 @llvm.umax.i32(i32 [[A]], i32 1)
; CHECK-NEXT:    call void @use(i32 [[ADD2]])
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 [[ADD2]]
  %add1 = add i32 %A, 1
  %icmp = icmp ne i32 %A, 0
  %sext = sext i1 %icmp to i32
  %add2 = add i32 %add1, %sext
  call void @use(i32 %add2)
  ret i32 %add2
}

issue.title: Missed optimization: while (len > 1) len -= 2;
issue.author: Alcaro

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
Missing fold: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/8aAHD-
After folding this pattern, InstCombine will combine the remaining expression into `(len & 1) != 0`: https://godbolt.org/z/hGvbnnvcd

comments.author: pinskia
comments.body: Note GCC misses the original code (while loop) optimization, that is filed as https://gcc.gnu.org/PR118316 but it also misses what LLVM misses for the LLVM code too: https://gcc.gnu.org/PR118317 .

comments.author: Ruhung
comments.body: I am interested in this issue. Could you assign it to me? Thank you.

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
> I am interested in this issue. Could you assign it to me? Thank you.

Please read https://llvm.org/docs/InstCombineContributorGuide.html before submitting the pr :)

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
> Reduced from an implementation of [this idea](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/main/libcxx/include/__charconv/tables.h#L143).

I'm not sure how it relates to the case you provided. Can you explain it further?

comments.author: Alcaro
comments.body:
The unreduced case was https://godbolt.org/z/a5W16x85x; lines 33-43 of the GCC output irritated me.

I can't reproduce the issue in Clang in a functional stringifier.

comments.author: v01dXYZ
comments.body:
It seems when we replace 2 by another power of two this was compiled as expected. Maybe 2 was a very special case.
When replacing by not a power of 2 (ie 3) with irem generated with a imul instead of bitwise-and, some useless instructions remain.
